title: M4: Visual terminal milestone
description: Acceptance: Window displays tabs and split panes with live terminal content. Tab switching works. Focus indicators visible. Status bar shows workspace and pane info.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: M4 acceptance — window displays tabs and panes with live terminal content
description: Acceptance verification: window shows tabs and split panes with live terminal content from host. Tab switching, focus indicators, status bar all visible. Spec: §37.5 M4
close_reason: Completed: M4 acceptance gate test validates all four §37.5 criteria — tabs, split panes with live ConPTY content, tab switching, focus indicators, and status bar

title: Another Bead [type:epic, priority:1, labels:slice]
description: Another description with **more detail**.

title: S2: CLI-driven workspace
description: Open a named workspace via wtd open, list panes by semantic name, send text, capture output, inspect metadata, and close — all driven by the wtd CLI. Proves workspace model, semantic naming, and controller round-trip.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: Verify wtd open finds definition, creates instance, lists panes by name
description: Integration verification: wtd open <name> discovers definition from CWD, creates workspace instance, wtd list panes shows all panes with semantic names. Spec: §12, §22.3
close_reason: Completed: Gate test in crates/wtd-cli/tests/gate_open_list_panes.rs — 4 tests verify CWD-based workspace discovery via find_workspace_in(), open creates WorkspaceInstance, list panes returns semantic names for single-pane and 3-pane split workspaces, plus error cases.

title: Gate: Verify wtd send and wtd capture work end-to-end
description: Integration verification: wtd send dev/server 'echo hello' delivers input, wtd capture dev/server returns output containing 'hello'. Proves CLI-to-host-to-session round-trip. Spec: §22.3
close_reason: Completed: 5 integration tests in gate_send_capture.rs verify CLI send/capture round-trip — single pane, multi-pane target isolation, nonexistent target errors, and --no-newline flag behavior

title: Gate: Verify all wtd commands with correct exit codes and JSON output
description: Comprehensive verification: all wtd subcommands tested, JSON output valid, exit codes match §22.9. This closes Slice 2. Spec: §22
close_reason: Completed: 7 gate tests covering all wtd subcommands — full lifecycle, exit codes §22.9 (0/2/3), JSON structure for all response types, cross-pane isolation, error JSON validity. S2 closed.

title: Another Bead [type:epic, priority:1]
description: Another description.

title: W9: Release packaging and CI
description: CI pipeline for build/test on every push. Tagged releases produce pre-compiled Windows binaries (wtd-host.exe, wtd-ui.exe, wtd.exe) published as GitHub Releases with SHA-256 checksums.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI workflow for Windows build and test
description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) that runs on push and pull request. Uses a windows-latest runner. Steps: checkout, install Rust stable MSVC, cargo build --workspace, cargo test --workspace. Cache cargo registry and target directory for speed. Expected outcome: every push and PR gets automated build and test feedback. Completion evidence: workflow runs green on current master.
close_reason: Completed: Added .github/workflows/ci.yml — triggers on push/PR to master, windows-latest runner, Rust stable MSVC, cargo build + test with registry/target caching

title: Configure release build profile and binary packaging script
description: Configure Cargo.toml release profile with LTO, strip symbols, and codegen-units=1 for optimized binaries. Create a packaging script (tools/package-release.sh or PowerShell equivalent) that builds in release mode, collects wtd-host.exe, wtd-ui.exe, and wtd.exe into a versioned archive (wtd-<version>-windows-x86_64.zip) with a bundled README and LICENSE. Expected outcome: running the script produces a self-contained release archive. Completion evidence: script produces a zip with all three binaries and they run correctly.
close_reason: Completed: Release profile configured (full LTO, codegen-units=1, strip symbols) and packaging script created. Script produces wtd-0.1.0-windows-x86_64.zip with all three binaries, README, and LICENSE. All binaries verified to run correctly.

title: Create GitHub Actions release workflow for publishing binaries
description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) triggered by version tags (v*). Builds release binaries on Windows runner, runs the packaging script, generates SHA-256 checksums, and creates a GitHub Release with the archive and checksum file attached. Uses the gh CLI or actions/create-release. Expected outcome: pushing a version tag automatically produces a GitHub Release with downloadable binaries. Completion evidence: test with a pre-release tag produces a draft release with correct artifacts.

title: M7: Runnable application
description: Milestone 7: The application is runnable, not just testable. wtd-host starts with a real request handler, workspaces load from YAML, CLI commands work end-to-end, UI clients receive live output, and actions modify workspace state.

title: Gate test for M7: Full application acceptance
description: Demonstrate full M7 acceptance: start wtd-host (real handler, not stub), open workspace from YAML file, verify sessions are running, send input and capture output via CLI, verify UI client receives live session output, invoke actions that modify layout, close workspace cleanly. This is the final gate proving the application works end-to-end. Expected outcome: WinTermDriver is a runnable application. Completion evidence: scripted test exercises the complete lifecycle and all commands succeed.
close_reason: Completed: M7 acceptance gate test with 3 test functions exercising full lifecycle — host start with real handler + broadcaster, workspace open from YAML, session verification, send/capture I/O, UI push delivery, action dispatch (split-right/close-pane), error paths, and clean workspace close.

title: M5: Interactive workspace milestone
description: Acceptance: Typing, keybindings, prefix chords, mouse, clipboard, and command palette all work.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: M5 acceptance — full interactive workspace functional
description: Acceptance verification: typing, keybindings, prefix chords (Ctrl+B,% splits), mouse click focus, text selection, copy/paste, command palette all work. Spec: §37.5 M5
close_reason: Completed: M5 acceptance gate test validates all six criteria — typing, single-stroke bindings, prefix chords, mouse focus, text selection/copy/paste, and command palette

title: Test Clean Title [type:epic, priority:1]
description: Description here.

title: W5: Rendering technology evaluation
description: A rendering technology is chosen for the terminal UI with benchmarks and a written decision document. A minimal prototype demonstrates VT output rendering in a window.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Evaluate candidate renderers with benchmarks and decision document
description: Time-boxed evaluation of wezterm components, Win32+DirectWrite, WebView2+xterm.js. Assess latency, memory, build complexity, embeddability. Written decision document with benchmarks and recommendation. Clear go/no-go for each candidate. Spec: §7.9
close_reason: Completed: Evaluated 3 rendering candidates with DirectWrite benchmark (5 modes, 500 frames each). Decision: Win32+DirectWrite selected. Decision doc at docs/decisions/001-rendering-technology.md.

title: Build minimal prototype rendering VT output in a window
description: Minimal prototype using chosen renderer. Creates window, displays VT output bytes, proves rendering path end-to-end. Basic attributes (colors, bold) render correctly. Foundation for W6 work. Spec: §7.9
close_reason: Completed: Minimal rendering prototype using Direct2D + DirectWrite. TerminalRenderer renders ScreenBuffer with colors, bold, italic, underline, inverse, dim, strikethrough, 256-color, and truecolor. 30 tests pass including end-to-end rendering pipeline.

title: Test Bead [type:task, priority:2]
description: A test bead description.

title: W10: Documentation and showcase
description: Complete README with run instructions and screenshots. Agent guidance document for driving terminal sessions programmatically through WinTermDriver.

title: Update README with complete run instructions and quickstart
description: Rewrite the README Run section now that the application is runnable. Include: prerequisites (Windows 10 1809+, Rust toolchain or pre-built binaries), installation from GitHub Releases (download, extract, add to PATH), first-run quickstart (start wtd-host, create a workspace YAML, wtd open, wtd send, wtd capture), a complete example workspace YAML with profiles, and troubleshooting tips (host not starting, pipe connection errors, ConPTY requirements). Remove the 'not yet runnable' note. Expected outcome: a new user can go from zero to a working terminal session by following the README. Completion evidence: a fresh clone following only README instructions successfully runs the full quickstart.

title: Write agent guidance document for driving WinTermDriver programmatically
description: Create docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md explaining how an AI agent (or any automation) can use WinTermDriver to manage terminal sessions. Cover: architecture overview (host/CLI/UI split), starting the host, workspace YAML authoring for automation, opening workspaces and naming panes semantically, sending commands via 'wtd send', capturing output via 'wtd capture' with timing considerations, inspecting pane state, invoking actions (split, close, resize), orchestrating multi-session workflows (e.g., run server in one pane, test in another, capture results), error handling and session restart, JSON output format for machine consumption, and best practices (polling intervals, output fencing with markers, cleanup). Include worked examples showing a complete agent workflow. Expected outcome: an agent developer can read this document and build a WinTermDriver-based automation. Completion evidence: document covers all wtd CLI commands with examples, and a sample workflow script is included.

title: Capture screenshots of running system and add to README
description: Capture high-quality screenshots of WinTermDriver in use and add them to the README. Screenshots should show: (1) a window with multiple tabs and split panes showing terminal output, (2) the command palette open with fuzzy search, (3) prefix chord indicator visible in the status bar, (4) a failed/exited pane with error display, (5) the status bar showing workspace name and pane path. Store images in docs/images/ as PNGs. Add to README with descriptive alt text and captions. Expected outcome: the README gives a visual impression of the application. Completion evidence: README renders correctly on GitHub with all images loading.

title: M2: CLI-driven workspace milestone
description: Acceptance: wtd open/list/send/capture/inspect/close all work. JSON output works. Exit codes are correct.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: M2 acceptance — all CLI commands work with correct output and exit codes
description: Acceptance verification: wtd open/list/send/capture/inspect/close all work. JSON output correct. Exit codes match spec. Spec: §37.5 M2
close_reason: Completed: M2 acceptance gate test in gate_m2_acceptance.rs validates all 7 criteria — open/list-panes/send/capture/inspect/close with text+JSON output formatting and exit codes

title: W3: Host process and IPC
description: A singleton per-user host process manages workspace instances, session lifecycles, and an IPC server. Actions from any source dispatch through a unified action system. The host is auto-startable, crash-recoverable, and cleanly shutdownable.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Define all IPC message types as Rust structs with serde round-trip tests
description:
Expected outcome:
- All message types from §13.14 defined as Rust structs with serde Serialize/Deserialize.
- Message envelope: id (UUID), type (string discriminator), payload (type-specific).
- Length-prefixed framing: 4-byte u32 LE length prefix + UTF-8 JSON payload.
- Maximum message size enforced (16 MiB).
- Error response type with code, message, and optional candidates.

Completion evidence:
- Round-trip serialization tests for every message type (serialize → deserialize → equality).
- Framing tests: encode/decode with length prefix.
- Oversize message rejection test.

Spec references: §13.4–13.14
close_reason: Completed: All 40+ IPC message types defined as Rust structs with serde Serialize/Deserialize, Envelope wire format (id + type + payload), length-prefixed binary framing (4-byte u32 LE, 16 MiB max), parse_envelope dispatcher, 67 round-trip and framing tests.

title: Create, monitor, and restart sessions with configurable backoff
description:
Expected outcome:
- Session manager creates sessions using the ConPTY wrapper with resolved launch specs.
- Monitors child process exit via process handle.
- Drains remaining PTY output on exit.
- Evaluates restart policy (never, on-failure, always) and schedules restarts with exponential backoff.
- Backoff: 500ms → 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 16s → 30s cap. Resets after 60s stable run.
- Startup command delivery: sends text + newline after 100ms delay.
- Session state machine: Creating → Running → Exited/Failed → Restarting.

Completion evidence:
- Integration test: create session, verify Running state, send input, read output.
- Integration test: session exit detected, exit code captured.
- Integration test: restart on failure with backoff delay progression.
- Unit test: backoff reset after stable run > 60s.
- Integration test: startup command delivered and visible in output.

Spec references: §17
close_reason: Completed: Session manager with state machine (Creating/Running/Exited/Failed/Restarting), restart policies (never/on-failure/always), exponential backoff (500ms-30s cap, reset after 60s stable), startup command delivery after 100ms, and exit code capture via GetExitCodeProcess. 12 tests pass (4 unit + 8 integration).

title: Create workspace instances from definitions with pane-session attachments
description:
Expected outcome:
- Workspace instance manager creates WorkspaceInstance from WorkspaceDefinition.
- Creates Windows Job Object per instance for child process cleanup.
- Depth-first traversal of layout tree creates one session per pane.
- Manages pane-to-session 1:1 attachments.
- Handles lifecycle state transitions: Creating → Active → Closing → Closed, and Recreating.
- Partial failure tolerance: if N/M sessions fail, M-N succeed and workspace opens.
- Supports open, attach, recreate, close, and save operations.

Completion evidence:
- Integration test: open workspace from fixture YAML, verify all sessions reach Running state.
- Integration test: partial failure (nonexistent executable), verify other sessions run, failed pane shows error.
- Integration test: close workspace, verify all sessions terminate and job object cleanup.
- Integration test: recreate workspace, verify fresh sessions.

Spec references: §26, §27.2, §29.2–29.3
close_reason: Completed: WorkspaceInstance manager with open/close/recreate/save, pane-session attachments, Job Object per instance, partial failure tolerance, LayoutTree::from_pane_node and to_pane_node, plus 14 tests including integration tests for all lifecycle operations

title: Accept named pipe IPC connections with handshake and message routing
description:
Expected outcome:
- Named pipe server at \.\pipe\wtd-{SID} using windows-rs and tokio.
- Pipe created with SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES granting access only to owning user SID.
- Client SID verification on each connection via GetNamedPipeClientProcessId + token inspection.
- Accepts multiple concurrent connections (UI and CLI clients).
- Handshake: client sends Handshake, host responds with HandshakeAck or Error.
- Request/response routing to workspace/session managers.
- Streaming pattern for UI output (SessionOutput push) and CLI follow.
- Host-to-UI notifications: SessionStateChanged, TitleChanged, LayoutChanged, WorkspaceStateChanged.

Completion evidence:
- Integration test: connect, handshake, send ListWorkspaces, receive response.
- Integration test: two concurrent clients connected simultaneously.
- Integration test: reject connection from different user (if testable).
- Integration test: streaming output to UI client.

Spec references: §13, §28
close_reason: Completed: Named pipe IPC server with SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES DACL, client SID verification, tokio accept loop, handshake protocol, RequestHandler routing, UI broadcast/streaming, and 6 integration tests all passing.

title: Register and dispatch actions through unified action system
description:
Expected outcome:
- Action registry with all v1 actions from §20.3 registered by name (kebab-case).
- Each action has: name, target type, typed arguments, description.
- Single ActionDispatcher processes actions from all sources identically.
- Actions wired to workspace/session managers for execution.
- Argument validation before dispatch.

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests: register actions, look up by name, validate arguments.
- Integration test: dispatch split-right action, verify layout tree modified.
- Integration test: dispatch close-pane action, verify session terminated and tree updated.
- Unit test: invalid action name returns error. Invalid arguments return error.

Spec references: §20
close_reason: Completed: Action registry with all 36 v1 actions, ActionDispatcher with argument validation, integration tests for split-right and close-pane

title: Enforce single-instance host with auto-start, PID file, job objects, and shutdown
description:
Expected outcome:
- Host process starts on-demand when CLI or UI first connects.
- Single-instance enforced by named pipe existence check.
- PID file written to %APPDATA%\WinTermDriver\host.pid on startup.
- Auto-start: CLI/UI launches wtd-host as DETACHED_PROCESS if pipe not found, polls for availability.
- Clean shutdown on wtd host stop, idle timeout, or termination signal.
- Shutdown sequence: notify UI clients, close all sessions, close pipe, remove PID file.
- Crash recovery: stale PID file detected and cleaned up on next start.

Completion evidence:
- Integration test: start host, verify pipe available, connect.
- Integration test: second host launch attempt detects existing and exits.
- Integration test: stop host, verify pipe gone, PID file removed.
- Integration test: auto-start from CLI client when host not running.

Spec references: §16
close_reason: Completed: Single-instance host enforcement, PID file management, auto-start from CLI/UI, console ctrl handler, graceful shutdown with cleanup, stale PID crash recovery. 6 integration tests covering all completion evidence.

title: S4: Interactive workspace
description: Type into panes, use keybindings, prefix chords, mouse, clipboard, and command palette. Proves the full interaction model.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: Verify typing produces output and single-stroke bindings dispatch actions
description: Integration verification: typing in pane produces session output, Ctrl+Shift+T creates new tab. Proves keyboard pipeline. Spec: §21.1
close_reason: Completed: 7 gate tests verifying keyboard pipeline — regular typing produces SendToSession bytes, Ctrl+Shift+T dispatches new-tab, multiple single-stroke bindings verified, binding vs raw input distinction, key_event_to_bytes correctness, prefix key activation, and full IPC round-trip with typed bytes reaching ConPTY session.

title: Gate: Verify prefix chords and mouse interactions work
description: Integration verification: Ctrl+B,% splits right, Ctrl+B,o cycles focus. Mouse click changes focus, splitter drag resizes. Spec: §21.3, §21.6
close_reason: Completed: 8 integration tests verifying prefix chords (Ctrl+B,% split-right; Ctrl+B,o focus-next-pane), mouse click focus change, splitter drag resize, and combined keyboard+mouse interaction

title: Gate: Verify command palette and clipboard operations work
description: Integration verification: palette opens, searches, dispatches actions. Copy/paste works with VT stripping and bracketed paste. Closes Slice 4. Spec: §24.6, §24.7
close_reason: Completed: 16 integration tests verifying command palette (open/search/dispatch/navigate/click/toggle/render overlay) and clipboard operations (VT stripping, selection extraction, bracketed paste DECSET 2004, clipboard round-trip). Closes Slice 4.

title: Second Title [type:task, priority:2]
description: Another desc.

title: Set up Cargo workspace with initial crate structure and shared dependencies
description:
Expected outcome:
- Cargo workspace at repo root with members for each planned crate (wtd-host, wtd-ui, wtd-cli, wtd-core, wtd-pty, wtd-ipc).
- Shared dependencies declared in workspace Cargo.toml (serde, thiserror, windows-rs).
- Each crate builds with cargo build (empty lib.rs or main.rs as appropriate).
- .gitignore updated for Rust artifacts (target/).
- rust-toolchain.toml pinning stable MSVC.

Completion evidence:
- cargo build --workspace succeeds.
- cargo test --workspace succeeds (no tests yet, just compiles).
- Crate layout matches architecture from spec §7.

Spec references: §7, §34
close_reason: Completed: Cargo workspace with 6 member crates (wtd-core, wtd-ipc, wtd-pty, wtd-host, wtd-ui, wtd-cli), shared workspace deps, rust-toolchain.toml; cargo build --workspace and cargo test --workspace pass (8 tests)

title: Test Bead
description: type: task

title: S1: Headless round-trip
description: Parse a workspace YAML file, resolve a profile, create a ConPTY session, send input, maintain a VT screen buffer, and capture visible screen state — all without a UI. First thin slice proving the core pipeline.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: Verify parsed workspace definition creates a running ConPTY session
description: Integration verification: load a fixture YAML, resolve profile, create workspace instance, verify session reaches Running state with a live ConPTY. This is the first integration point connecting W1 and W3. Spec: §17.1
close_reason: Completed: YAML fixture → load_workspace_definition → WorkspaceInstance::open → sessions reach Running with live ConPTY. Three integration tests: single-pane, split-pane, and startup command output verification.

title: Gate: Verify input sent to session appears in VT screen buffer output
description: Integration verification: send text to a running session PTY input, read output from PTY, feed to screen buffer, assert screen state contains expected text. Proves the I/O pipeline. Spec: §14.4
close_reason: Completed: Two integration tests verify I/O round-trip — write_input() to session, process_pending_output() drains to ScreenBuffer, visible_text() contains expected markers. Sequential ordering also verified.

title: Gate: Verify full headless round-trip via IPC — open, send, capture
description: End-to-end verification: connect to host via IPC, open workspace, send input to session, capture visible screen, assert output matches. This closes Slice 1. Spec: §23.1
close_reason: Completed: Full headless round-trip via IPC verified — connect, handshake, open workspace, send input, capture screen, assert output matches. Closes Slice 1.

title: W8: Host runtime wiring
description: Wire independently-tested components into a working host process. Replace StubHandler with real HostRequestHandler, broadcast session I/O to UI clients, load workspaces at runtime, populate AttachWorkspace state, add CLI timeouts, and wire action dispatch.

title: Replace StubHandler with real HostRequestHandler
description: Replace the StubHandler in wtd-host/src/main.rs with a real HostRequestHandler that owns WorkspaceManager, SessionManager, and ActionDispatcher. Dispatch all IPC request types (OpenWorkspace, AttachWorkspace, Send, Capture, InvokeAction, ListWorkspaces, ListPanes, InspectPane, CloseWorkspace) to the appropriate component. Use the GateHandler in tests/gate_ipc_round_trip.rs as a reference implementation. Expected outcome: host responds to all IPC message types with real results instead of returning None. Completion evidence: integration test sends OpenWorkspace + Send + Capture via IPC and receives correct responses. Spec refs: §8.1, §13.9-13.13.
close_reason: Completed: HostRequestHandler replaces StubHandler in main.rs, dispatches all IPC message types to real WorkspaceInstance/Session/ActionDispatcher. Integration test verifies full round-trip: OpenWorkspace+Send+Capture via IPC.

title: Wire session I/O broadcasting to UI clients
description: Add a background task that drains ConPTY output from each session's reader thread, feeds it to the session's ScreenBuffer, and broadcasts SessionOutput messages to all connected UI clients via IpcServer::broadcast_to_ui. Also broadcast SessionStateChanged and TitleChanged events when session state transitions occur. Expected outcome: UI clients receive live terminal output without polling. Completion evidence: integration test starts a session, writes input, and verifies a connected UI client receives SessionOutput messages via IPC push. Spec refs: §13.10, §14.2.
close_reason: Completed: Added output broadcaster that drains ConPTY output every 50ms, feeds to ScreenBuffers, and broadcasts SessionOutput/SessionStateChanged/TitleChanged to UI clients. Integration tests verify UI client receives live push messages.

title: Populate AttachWorkspaceResult with full workspace state
description: When the host handles AttachWorkspace, serialize the WorkspaceInstance's current state into AttachWorkspaceResult — including layout tree (windows, tabs, pane splits), session IDs and their states (Running, Exited, Failed, Restarting), and pane-session attachments. Currently returns an empty JSON object. Expected outcome: UI clients can initialize their view from the attach response. Completion evidence: integration test creates a workspace with multiple panes, attaches, and verifies the returned state contains the correct layout tree and session IDs. Spec refs: §13.12.
close_reason: Completed: AttachWorkspaceResult now returns serialized AttachSnapshot with layout tree (PaneNode), session states, pane-session attachments, and session titles. Added Serialize to SessionState, WorkspaceState, PaneState, AttachSnapshot, TabSnapshot. 3 integration tests verify multi-pane and single-pane attach responses.

title: Wire runtime workspace loading from file path
description: When OpenWorkspace includes a file path, read the file, call load_workspace_definition to parse and validate the YAML, resolve profiles, create a WorkspaceInstance, spawn ConPTY sessions for each pane, and register the instance in the host's workspace registry. Handle errors (file not found, parse failure, profile resolution failure) with structured IPC error responses. Expected outcome: 'wtd open --file my-workspace.yaml' creates a running workspace with live sessions. Completion evidence: integration test provides a YAML file path via OpenWorkspace, verifies the workspace is created and sessions are running. Spec refs: §8.1, §10.3.
close_reason: Completed: OpenWorkspace with explicit file path works end-to-end (load, parse, validate, create WorkspaceInstance with live ConPTY sessions). Added DefinitionError error code for parse/validation failures. Implemented SaveWorkspace file writing. 6 integration tests cover happy path and error cases.

title: Add CLI request timeout protection
description: Wrap read_frame_async in the CLI IPC client with tokio::time::timeout (default 30 seconds). Add a new ClientError::RequestTimeout variant with a clear error message. Expose a --timeout flag on all CLI commands to override the default. Expected outcome: CLI commands fail gracefully with a timeout error instead of hanging forever when the host is unresponsive. Completion evidence: unit test verifies that a request to a non-responding server times out after the configured duration. Spec refs: §9.2.
close_reason: Completed: Added RequestTimeout variant to ClientError, wrapped read_frame_async with tokio::time::timeout (30s default), exposed --timeout global CLI flag, integration test verifies timeout on unresponsive server.

title: Wire ActionDispatcher into host request handler
description: In the HostRequestHandler, handle InvokeAction messages by resolving the target workspace and pane, calling ActionDispatcher::dispatch, processing the ActionResult (PaneCreated, PaneClosed, FocusMoved, etc.), and returning success or a structured error via IPC. Expected outcome: 'wtd action split-right --target dev/editor' splits a pane in the running workspace via IPC. Completion evidence: integration test sends InvokeAction via IPC for split-right and verifies the layout tree now has an additional pane. Spec refs: §18.1-18.3.
close_reason: Completed: Wired ActionDispatcher into HostRequestHandler — InvokeAction now processes ActionResult (PaneCreated spawns session, PaneClosed cleans up), returns structured InvokeActionResult via IPC. 4 integration tests verify split-right, close-pane, focus-next, and error handling.

title: M1: Headless round-trip milestone
description: Acceptance: workspace YAML parsed, ConPTY session launched, input sent via IPC, screen buffer populated, capture returns expected output. No UI required.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: M1 acceptance — headless workspace round-trip demonstrated
description: Acceptance verification: workspace YAML parsed, ConPTY session launched, input sent via IPC, screen buffer populated, capture returns expected output. No UI required. Spec: §37.5 M1
close_reason: Completed: M1 acceptance gate test validates all §37.5 criteria — YAML parse, profile resolve, ConPTY launch, IPC send, screen buffer populate, capture returns expected output. 244 workspace tests pass.

close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: Verify terminal content from host renders in a pane viewport
description: Integration verification: UI connects to host, receives SessionOutput, terminal content renders in pane. Proves UI rendering pipeline. Spec: §24.4
close_reason: Completed: Gate test verifies UI IPC connect, SessionOutput push reception, ScreenBuffer population, and Direct2D pane viewport rendering (plain text + ANSI colors)

title: Gate: Verify window shows tab strip and split panes with focus indicators
description: Integration verification: window displays tabs and split panes, tab switching works, focus indicator visible on active pane. Spec: §24.2-24.4

title: Gate: Verify status bar and failed pane display work
description: Integration verification: status bar shows workspace/pane info, failed pane shows error with restart prompt. Closes Slice 3. Spec: §24.5, §24.8
close_reason: Completed: 8 integration tests verifying status bar (workspace name, pane path, all session states, prefix indicator) and failed/exited pane overlays with restart hints, including full composited frames.

title: M3: Rendering spike complete milestone

close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: M3 acceptance — rendering decision documented, prototype renders VT output
description: Acceptance verification: decision document written with benchmarks, prototype renders VT output in a window using chosen renderer. Spec: §37.5 M3
close_reason: Completed: M3 acceptance gate test validates decision document (benchmarks, three candidates, GO verdict) and prototype rendering (plain + styled VT output via Direct2D/DirectWrite)

title: W2: Terminal core
description: ConPTY sessions can be created, receive input, and produce VT output. Output is parsed into a queryable screen buffer with scrollback. A binary pane layout tree supports split, close, resize, and focus traversal.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Create, resize, and close ConPTY pseudo-consoles and child processes
description:
Expected outcome:
- Rust wrapper around CreatePseudoConsole that handles the full lifecycle: create pipes, create pseudo-console, configure STARTUPINFOEX, CreateProcess, close child-side pipe ends, start I/O loops.
- Resize via ResizePseudoConsole works correctly.
- Clean close: close ConPTY handle, terminate child process, close pipe handles.
- Process tree management via Windows Job Objects.

Completion evidence:
- Integration test: launch pwsh.exe, send "echo hello\r\n", read output bytes containing "hello", close cleanly.
- Integration test: resize during active session, verify no crash.
- Integration test: close ConPTY, verify child process terminates.
- Test on minimum supported version (build 17763) if available.

Spec references: §14.1–14.3, §14.6
close_reason: Completed: Implemented full ConPTY lifecycle (create/resize/close pseudo-console, child process spawn via STARTUPINFOEXW, anonymous pipe I/O, Job Object kill-on-close). Fixed STARTF_USESTDHANDLES+INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE to prevent child from inheriting parent console. All 5 integration tests pass.

title: Parse VT byte stream into queryable screen state with scrollback ring buffer
description:
Expected outcome:
- Screen buffer with active grid (cols × rows), scrollback ring of configurable depth, cursor tracking, and alternate screen support.
- VT parser (vte crate) feeds bytes into the screen buffer state machine.
- Cell structure: character, fg/bg color (ANSI + 256 + truecolor), attributes (bold, italic, underline, etc.), wide character support.
- Scrollback ring discards oldest rows when full.
- Title extraction from OSC sequences.
- Queryable: read cell at position, read row as text, read visible screen as text (for capture command).

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests: feed known VT sequences, assert cell contents, cursor position, colors, attributes.
- Unit tests: alternate screen activation/deactivation preserves primary screen.
- Unit tests: scrollback fills and wraps correctly.
- Unit tests: title extraction from \e]0;title\a sequences.
- Unit tests: wide character handling.

Spec references: §14.4, §14.5, §15
close_reason: Completed: VT screen buffer in wtd-pty::screen — active grid, alternate screen, scrollback ring, cursor, SGR (8/256/truecolor/attrs), wide chars, title from OSC; 28 unit tests all pass.

title: Split, close, resize, and traverse focus in binary pane layout tree
description:
Expected outcome:
- Binary tree data structure for pane layouts: leaf nodes (panes) and split nodes (orientation, ratio, two children).
- Split operations: split-right and split-down replace a leaf with a split node containing the original and a new pane.
- Close operations: remove leaf, promote sibling, collapse parent split.
- Resize: adjust ratio of split nodes, clamp to minimum pane size (2 cols × 1 row).
- Focus traversal: next/prev (depth-first wrapping), directional (up/down/left/right using geometric center).
- Zoom: toggle a pane to fill tab area, hiding siblings.

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests: split a single pane right and down, verify tree structure.
- Unit tests: close pane, verify sibling promotion.
- Unit tests: resize with ratio clamping at min size.
- Unit tests: focus traversal in all directions on a 4-pane layout.
- Unit tests: zoom/unzoom preserves layout.

Spec references: §18
close_reason: Completed: Binary pane layout tree in wtd-core::layout with arena-backed LayoutTree supporting split right/down, close with sibling promotion, resize with min-size clamping, depth-first and directional focus traversal, and zoom toggle. 18 unit tests.

title: M6: Validated release milestone
description: Acceptance: All acceptance criteria (§36) pass. Performance targets (§30) met. Logging operational. Error messages clear and complete.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Gate: M6 acceptance — all acceptance criteria from §36 pass
description: Final acceptance: all 10 acceptance criteria from §36 demonstrated. Workspace lifecycle, mixed sessions, manual interaction, controller, naming, chords, partial failure, security, workspace-as-code, recreation determinism. Spec: §36
close_reason: Completed: M6 acceptance gate test validates all 10 §36 criteria — workspace lifecycle, mixed sessions, manual interaction, controller interaction, semantic naming, prefix chords, partial failure tolerance, local security, workspace-as-code, and recreation determinism.

title: Gate: M6 performance — performance targets from §30 met
description: Performance validation: keystroke-to-echo <50ms, rendering <16ms/frame, capture <100ms, workspace open <2s for 5 sessions, 100MB/s throughput, 20+ concurrent sessions. Spec: §30
close_reason: Completed: 6 performance gate tests validating all §30 targets — capture <100ms, keystroke echo <50ms, workspace open <2s (5 sessions), throughput 100MB/s, frame advance <16ms, 24 concurrent sessions

title: Gate: M6 diagnostics — logging operational, error messages complete
description: Diagnostics validation: tracing infrastructure operational with rotation, log levels configurable, all error messages reviewed for clarity. Spec: §31, §29
close_reason: Completed: tracing infrastructure (wtd-core::logging) with file+stderr host logging, stderr CLI/UI logging, WTD_LOG env override, and 24-test gate covering §31 logging and §29 error message clarity

title: S5: End-to-end integration
description: Slice 5 validates that all independently-tested components are wired into a working application. Gate beads verify the full lifecycle: host starts with real handler, workspaces load from YAML, CLI commands work end-to-end, UI clients receive live output, and actions modify workspace state.

title: Gate test for S5: CLI-to-host round-trip with real handler
description: Verify the full CLI-to-host round-trip works with the real HostRequestHandler (not StubHandler or test harness). Start wtd-host, open a workspace from a YAML file via 'wtd open', send input via 'wtd send', capture output via 'wtd capture', and verify the captured output matches. Verify 'wtd list', 'wtd inspect', and 'wtd close' all return correct results. This is the gate test for Slice 5 headless integration. Expected outcome: all wtd CLI commands work against a real host process. Completion evidence: test script exercises open/send/capture/list/inspect/close in sequence against a real host.
close_reason: Completed: 5 integration tests in gate_s5_cli_to_host.rs verify full CLI-to-host round-trip with real HostRequestHandler — open/send/capture/list/inspect/close with text and JSON output formatting

title: Gate test for S5: UI client receives live session output
description: Verify that a UI client connected to the host receives live SessionOutput broadcasts when a session produces output. Start host, open workspace, attach UI client, send input to a session, and verify the UI client receives SessionOutput messages via the IPC push channel. Also verify SessionStateChanged notifications when a session exits. Expected outcome: UI clients see real-time terminal output without polling. Completion evidence: integration test verifies push messages arrive at UI client within expected latency.
close_reason: Completed: 4 integration tests in gate_s5_ui_session_output.rs verify UI client receives live SessionOutput broadcasts (marker text, state-changed on exit, latency under 2s, multi-client broadcast)

title: Gate test for S5: Action dispatch modifies workspace via IPC
description: Verify that actions dispatched via CLI (wtd action split-right, split-down, close-pane, etc.) modify the workspace layout via the real host request handler. Open a workspace, invoke split-right via IPC, verify the layout tree now has two panes. Invoke close-pane, verify the layout returns to one pane. Expected outcome: IPC-dispatched actions have real effects on workspace state. Completion evidence: integration test verifies layout tree changes after each action dispatch.
close_reason: Completed: 5 gate tests verify IPC-dispatched actions (split-right, split-down, close-pane, focus-next/prev-pane, error path) modify workspace layout state through the real HostRequestHandler

title: W6: UI rendering pipeline
description: Terminal content renders in panes within windows with tabs. The UI connects to the host via IPC, receives VT output streams, and displays them. Tab strip, pane splitters, status bar, and failed pane states are all rendered.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Create native windows with tab strip supporting switching, create, close, reorder
description: Native Windows window with title bar (workspace + active tab name). Tab strip: named tabs, active highlighted, click to switch, close button, creation, drag reorder, overflow handling. Last tab close closes window. Spec: §24.2-24.3
close_reason: Completed: Tab strip component with Direct2D rendering, mouse interaction (switch/create/close/reorder), overflow scroll arrows, drag-to-reorder, window title management. 20 unit + 14 integration tests. Renderer extended with compositing API.

title: Render pane layout areas with splitter bars, borders, and focus indicators
description: Tab content area renders pane layout tree as split panes. Draggable splitter bars. Focused pane has distinct border. Minimum pane size enforced (2 cols x 1 row). Spec: §24.4
close_reason: Completed: PaneLayout component with splitter bars, borders, focus indicators, and mouse drag interaction. 15 unit tests + 27 integration tests (4 with D2D rendering).

title: Render terminal content in pane viewports with cursor, colors, and attributes
description: Character grid from VT output stream. Cursor (block/underline/bar) with visibility. 8/16/256/truecolor support. Bold, italic, underline, inverse, strikethrough. Alternate screen buffer. Text selection highlight. TUI apps render without artifacts. Spec: §24.4, §14.5
close_reason: Completed: Per-pane viewport rendering with D2D clipping, cursor shape support (block/underline/bar via DECSCUSR), text selection highlight, 8/16/256/truecolor + all attributes. 36 new tests.

title: Connect UI to host via IPC: attach, receive output, send input, notifications
description: UI client connects via named pipe, handshake with clientType=ui. AttachWorkspace returns full state. Receives SessionOutput push events. Sends SessionInput and PaneResize. Receives SessionStateChanged, TitleChanged, LayoutChanged notifications. Spec: §13.9-13.13
notes: RERUN NEEDED: Previous run barely started (5 log lines, 3s) due to nested-runner bug. Agent did no real work. Full implementation required.
close_reason: Completed: UI IPC client (host_client, host_bridge modules) connects via named pipe with clientType=ui handshake, AttachWorkspace, receives SessionOutput/SessionStateChanged/TitleChanged/LayoutChanged push events, sends SessionInput/PaneResize/InvokeAction commands. 9 integration tests. main.rs integrated with --workspace flag.

title: Display failed and exited pane states with error and restart prompt
description: Detached pane shows centered error/exit message with restart prompt. Pane remains in layout. Restart action works from failed state. Spec: §24.5
close_reason: Completed: Added paint_failed_pane() to TerminalRenderer with centered error/exit message and restart hint overlay, message formatting helpers, and 9 tests (3 unit + 6 integration)

title: Render status bar with workspace name, pane path, prefix indicator, session state
description: Bottom status bar: workspace name, focused pane path, session state. Prefix-active indicator visible during chord entry. Updates on focus change. Spec: §24.8
close_reason: Completed: StatusBar component in wtd_ui::status_bar with workspace name, pane path, prefix-active badge, and color-coded session state. 12 unit tests pass.

title: W4: CLI controller
description: The wtd CLI can drive the host for all specified commands with human-readable and JSON output, structured exit codes, and semantic target addressing.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Parse and validate all wtd CLI commands with subcommands and flags
description:
Expected outcome:
- clap-based CLI parser for all wtd commands: open, attach, recreate, close, save, list (workspaces/instances/panes/sessions), focus, rename, action, send, keys, capture, scrollback, follow, inspect, host status, host stop.
- Global flags: --json, --verbose, --id, --help, --version.
- Typed arguments with validation (e.g., target paths, key specs).
- Shell completion generation.

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests: each subcommand parses valid arguments correctly.
- Unit tests: invalid arguments produce helpful error messages.
- Unit tests: --help produces correct usage text for each command.

Spec references: §22.1–22.3
close_reason: Completed: clap-based CLI parser for all 20 wtd commands with global flags, shell completions, and 59 unit tests

title: Resolve semantic target paths to runtime workspace objects
description:
Expected outcome:
- Target path parser handles 1–4 segment paths (pane, workspace/pane, workspace/tab/pane, workspace/window/tab/pane).
- Resolution against in-memory workspace instance graph.
- Implicit workspace when exactly one instance is active.
- Ambiguity detection with candidate list in error response.
- Internal UUID addressing via --id flag.

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests: resolve 1-segment path to pane in single-instance scenario.
- Unit tests: resolve 2-segment workspace/pane path.
- Unit tests: resolve 3 and 4-segment paths.
- Unit tests: ambiguous target returns candidates.
- Unit tests: zero or multiple instances with 1-segment path returns error.

Spec references: §19
close_reason: Completed: Target path parser (1-4 segments with §19.1 validation) and resolver against workspace instances (implicit/explicit workspace, tab/pane lookup, ambiguity detection, --id addressing). 19 unit tests covering all resolution scenarios.

title: Connect to host, send commands, receive responses, and format output
description:
Expected outcome:
- CLI IPC client connects to host named pipe, performs handshake.
- Sends command messages, receives responses.
- Formats output: human-readable text (default) or JSON (--json flag).
- Structured exit codes per §22.9.
- Auto-start trigger: if host not running, launch wtd-host and retry connection.
- Follow command maintains connection for streaming output.

Completion evidence:
- Integration test: connect to running host, send ListWorkspaces, format text output.
- Integration test: --json flag produces valid JSON output.
- Integration test: exit codes match expected values for success, not-found, ambiguous.
- Integration test: auto-start triggers host launch when pipe not found.

Spec references: §22.4–22.9
close_reason: Completed: CLI IPC client connects to host, sends commands, receives responses, formats text/JSON output with structured exit codes. Auto-start triggers host launch. Follow command streams output.

title: Verify all wtd commands end-to-end against running host
description:
Expected outcome:
- Comprehensive end-to-end test suite running each wtd subcommand against a real host with test workspace fixtures.
- Tests use TestHost harness with test-specific named pipe name.
- Each command tested: open, list panes, send, capture, scrollback, follow, inspect, close.
- JSON output validated for structure.
- Exit codes asserted for success, not-found, ambiguous, parse-error scenarios.
- Concurrent input test (CLI + simulated UI simultaneously).

Completion evidence:
- All wtd subcommands tested with at least one success and one error case.
- JSON output is valid and matches expected schema.
- Exit codes match §22.9 for each scenario.
- No crash or hang under concurrent input.

Spec references: §32.2
close_reason: Completed: 27 E2E tests covering all wtd subcommands (open, list, send, capture, scrollback, follow, inspect, close, attach, recreate, save, focus, rename, action, keys) with success/error cases, JSON output validation, exit code assertions per §22.9, and concurrent input test

title: W1: Workspace definition and configuration
description: A YAML workspace file can be loaded, validated, and its profiles resolved to concrete launch specs. Global settings can be loaded and merged. Workspace files can be discovered from the CWD or user directory.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Parse YAML workspace definition into validated WorkspaceDefinition model
description:
Expected outcome:
- The application can load a YAML workspace definition file and produce a validated WorkspaceDefinition struct.
- Validation covers: version field, name format, windows/tabs mutual exclusivity, unique tab/pane names, valid profile references, valid focus references, split node child count, ratio ranges.
- Invalid files produce clear errors with file path, field path, and human-readable message.

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests pass for valid fixture files (minimal and full examples from §10.4–10.5).
- Unit tests pass for each validation rule in §10.3 with targeted invalid fixtures.
- Round-trip: parse → serialize → parse produces identical structures.

Spec references: §9.1, §10
close_reason: Completed: WorkspaceDefinition type tree + load_workspace_definition with 10 validation rules; 24 tests pass including minimal/full fixtures and round-trip.

title: Resolve profiles from definition and global settings into concrete launch specs
description:
Expected outcome:
- Given a workspace definition, global settings, and a session launch definition, produce a fully resolved launch spec: executable path, arguments, working directory, and merged environment.
- Resolution follows the chain: session → workspace defaults → global settings → built-in defaults.
- All five profile types (powershell, cmd, wsl, ssh, custom) resolve correctly with their type-specific defaults.
- Environment merge handles null removal correctly.
- Environment variable expansion works in cwd paths (e.g., %USERPROFILE%).

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests cover all profile types with their default executables and arguments.
- Unit tests cover the full fallback chain (session → workspace → global → built-in).
- Unit tests cover environment merge with null removal across layers.
- Unit tests cover pwsh.exe → powershell.exe fallback for PowerShell type.

Spec references: §25
close_reason: Completed: GlobalSettings type + profile_resolver module; all 5 profile types with defaults, pwsh fallback, §25.1 lookup chain, §25.3 env merge with null removal; 29 new unit tests, 52 total pass

title: Load and validate global settings file with merge precedence
description:
Expected outcome:
- The application can load the global settings file from %APPDATA%\WinTermDriver\settings.yaml.
- Missing file produces sensible built-in defaults.
- Settings merge precedence: built-in → global file → workspace definition → per-session overrides.
- Keybinding merge: workspace chords/keys override global for same key, unoverridden bindings preserved.
- Font and theme configuration loaded with defaults.

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests cover missing file (defaults applied).
- Unit tests cover partial overrides (some fields set, others default).
- Unit tests cover keybinding merge precedence with workspace overrides.
- Unit tests cover font/theme defaults.

Spec references: §11
notes: RERUN NEEDED: Previous run completed the code (commit 67bb9ba) but agent called 'bash bead-runner.sh close' instead of 'br close', so the bead was never actually closed. Code may already be present — verify before reimplementing.
close_reason: Completed: Global settings loader with defaults, partial YAML, font/theme config, and keybinding merge precedence — all 15 tests passing (code from commit 67bb9ba already merged)

title: Discover workspace definition files from CWD and user directory
description:
Expected outcome:
- Given a workspace name, search for the definition file in order: explicit --file path, CWD .wtd/ directory, user workspace directory (%APPDATA%\WinTermDriver\workspaces\).
- First match wins. Supports .yaml, .yml, .json extensions.
- wtd list workspaces scans both CWD .wtd/ and user directory, showing source labels.
- Directory creation on first use.

Completion evidence:
- Unit tests cover search order with fixtures in temp directories.
- Unit tests cover explicit --file path (found and not found).
- Unit tests cover listing from both sources with correct labels.

Spec references: §12
close_reason: Completed: workspace_discovery module in wtd-core with find_workspace, list_workspaces, ensure_dir — search order (explicit/local/user), extension priority, 12 unit tests all passing

title: Test Bead [type:task, priority:2, labels:W1]
description: A test bead description.

title: W7: UI interaction model
description: The terminal workspace is fully interactive. Keyboard input, prefix chords, mouse interaction, clipboard, and command palette are all functional.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Classify keyboard input and route: raw input, bindings, or prefix
description: Input classifier: prefix key match, chord key match (if prefix active), single-stroke binding match, or raw terminal input. Raw forwarded as bytes. Bindings dispatch actions. Conflict resolution per §21.4. Spec: §21.1, §21.4
close_reason: Completed: Input classifier module with KeySpec parsing, 4-category classification (prefix/chord/single-stroke/raw), §21.4 conflict resolution, raw terminal byte conversion, and Win32 keyboard event capture. 61 tests.

title: Implement prefix chord state machine with timeout and visual indicator
description: Prefix key (Ctrl+B) enters PrefixActive. Chord key dispatches action. Escape/timeout cancel. Double-prefix sends literal. Unbound key sends prefix+key to session. Visual indicator in status bar. Spec: §21.3, §27.4
close_reason: Completed: PrefixStateMachine in wtd_ui::prefix_state — manages Idle/PrefixActive transitions (chord dispatch, double-prefix literal, Escape cancel, unbound key forwarding, timeout). 17 tests pass.

title: Handle mouse: click focus, selection, splitter drag, scroll, reporting
description: Left click focuses pane. Drag selects text. Splitter drag resizes. Right click paste/menu. Scroll wheel navigates scrollback. Mouse reporting forwarded as VT sequences when enabled. Spec: §21.6
close_reason: Completed: MouseHandler with focus, selection, scroll, paste, splitter drag, and VT mouse reporting (SGR+legacy). MouseMode tracking in ScreenBuffer. Extended MouseEventKind. 38 tests.

title: Implement copy and paste with VT stripping and bracketed paste support
description: Copy: selected text to clipboard, VT stripped. Paste: clipboard to session input. Bracketed paste wrapping when enabled. Copy-on-select configurable. Spec: §24.7
close_reason: Completed: Clipboard module with text extraction, VT stripping, Win32 clipboard read/write, bracketed paste wrapping (DECSET 2004), and 23 tests

title: Build command palette with fuzzy search and action dispatch
description: Modal overlay (Ctrl+Shift+Space). Fuzzy search over actions. Action list with keybinding hints. Target selection for targeted actions. Escape dismisses. Dispatches through unified action system. Spec: §24.6
close_reason: Completed: Command palette with fuzzy search, keybinding hints, keyboard/mouse interaction, and action dispatch via IPC. 36 v1 actions listed. 14 unit tests for fuzzy matching, keybinding hints, and entry building. All workspace tests pass.

title: M8: Shipped and documented
description: Milestone 8: Pre-compiled binaries published as GitHub Releases. README has complete run instructions with screenshots. Agent guidance document explains how to drive terminal sessions programmatically.

title: Gate test for M8: Release, docs, and showcase complete
description: Verify M8 acceptance: (1) a GitHub Release exists with pre-compiled binaries and checksums, (2) README contains complete run instructions that work from a fresh download, (3) README includes screenshots of the running application, (4) docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md exists with worked examples for programmatic use. Expected outcome: WinTermDriver is shipped and documented. Completion evidence: manual verification of all four criteria.
